content.full_text:
October 21, 1991

Santana For Supervisor
I.D. # 890817
405 Humboldt Way
Livermore, Ca 94550
510-447-2239

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street
Sacramento, Ca 95814

On April 15, 1991 Supervisor Charles Santana of District 2, Alameda
County, passed away. At the time of his death he left approximately
thirty thousand dollars in his "Santana For Supervisor" Committee account.

As treasurer of this account I contacted your legal division for advice as
to the proper way to eventually disperse this money. Your department was
very helpful and prompt with answers to my questions. The committee is
now ready to proceed with this matter and is prepared to donate the funds
to suitable charities. It is with regards to this that I write you now.

Some opposition has been raised by the late Supervisors heirs as to the
control of these funds. This committee is very clear as to the law in this
matter as we have been verbally advised by your department. What we
request are some written guidelines for terminating this committee and
dispersing these funds so that our decisions in this matter will be
respected.

We appreciated your help in this matter, and thank you in advance for your
assistance.

Sincerely,

Anthony Mastrantonio
Treasurer, Santana For Supervisor

[The image appears to be a very faded or low-contrast document. While I can see there is text present, the majority of the content is too illegible to transcribe accurately. Only a few partial fragments are barely visible at the top and in scattered areas of the page, but they are not clear enough to read with confidence.]

[illegible]
